title: taraxacum asturiense
intro: Taraxacum asturiense, commonly known as the Asturian dandelion, is a plant species within the dandelion genus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Taraxacum asturiense include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and support liver health, as well as its anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots can also be used in cooking and herbal preparations to add nutritional value and flavor.
